The source map, while potentially exposing source code, is standard during development but could be risky if exposed in production.\n\n- **Errors or Flaws in the Reports?**  \n  The reports are thorough and consistent. They correctly identify that the source map is embedded as a data URI, which is common but can be a security concern if source maps are accessible publicly.\n\n- **Scores and Justifications:**\n\n  - **Malware Score:**  \n    All reports assign a malware score of 0, which is appropriate. There is no malicious code or behavior.\n\n  - **Obfuscated Score:**  \n    All assign 0, which is correct; the code is straightforward, and the source map is not obfuscated but embedded as a data URI.\n\n  - **Security Risk Score:**  \n    Slightly varying but generally low (0.1 to 0.2).
